summary: Crystal‑clear audience tape captures a mellow 1979 show with a stellar Peggy‑O and an electrifying Other One > Dark Star jam, despite a shaky first set.
review: The recording is frequently praised as one of the best AUD tapes – crisp, full‑bodied and surprisingly balanced for a crowd source.  Musically the night is uneven: the first set suffers from rushed tempos and weak vocals (notably on Promised Land and Stagger Lee), while the second half shines with Peggy‑O, an inspiring Other One > Dark Star sequence, Jack Straw and a powerful It’s All Over Now.  Overall the tape delivers a rewarding listening experience, especially for fans who love the late‑’70s era.

ai_rating.rationale: The vast majority of reviews (≈80%) give five stars and extol both the recording fidelity and key performances; a few critical notes on the first set lower the show rating modestly, resulting in an overall strong but not perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear, lush and well‑balanced with a warm color and full low‑end.  Reviewers note a tiny loss of top‑end sparkle and an early volume dip on the first song, but overall the tape is described as one of the best audience recordings available.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as unfocused, rushed and with weak vocal performances; the band regroups after intermission, delivering a relaxed, high‑energy second set that includes several highlight jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone and vocals are repeatedly praised for sweetness and clarity, though one reviewer notes an off‑tempo intro and generally weak vocals early in the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s bass is highlighted as full‑force and a key driver of The Other One jam; reviewers appreciate its prominence compared to typical 1979 SBD recordings.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir receives mixed feedback – strong on It’s All Over Now and other covers, but his vocals are called weak on Promised Land and some early numbers.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards are largely absent from the mix; reviewers comment that Keith and Donna are barely audible or missing entirely, giving the night a quasi‑quintet feel.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum solos and subsequent percussive jam (marimba‑type) are praised for their power and groove.
